calendar.matters:
Petition For Instructions Regarding The Disposition Of Items Of Personal Property, Abatement Of General Gifts Pursuant To Probate Code Section 21400, Et Seq, And Final Distribution Of Trust Assets
Off Calendar Jan-10-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Yulissa Zulaica and Jennifer Ramirez for Pearl Chang-Lee (Trustee/Petitioner).

Ms. Ramirez addressed the issues raised in the Examiner's notes stating that they have not received any objection to the petition, referred to the memorandum describing the Indian bracelet, stated that the Testator's intent was for the Indian bracelet to go to Julia Fisher not Julia Schaefer, spoke of the Trustee's duty to carry out the Testator's intent, and proposed either distributing the bracelet to Julia Fisher or selling the bracelet and distributing the proceeds to Grateful Dogs Rescue. Ms. Zulaica proposed giving the bracelet to Grateful Dogs and letting them sell it in order to prevent further administration costs. Ms. Ramirez also made reference to Probate Code 21400 and the re-statement of the first amendment, stated that seven out of the eight beneficiaries are non-relatives and are receiving more than the relative, and made reference to exhibit C showing that Tina's share is being reduced and the difference is being given to a friend. The petition for instructions regarding disposition of items of personal property, abatement of general gifts pursuant to Probate Code 21400 and final distribution of trust assets was taken under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported.
